svg.aria-label: CV output stage perfboard placement map on a square 2.54 pitch grid
svg.text: CV output stage perfboard placement map
Main board is snapped to one square 2.54-style pitch in both directions so the DIP-8 socket and the pad field use the same spacing. Parts are drawn to land on pad multiples.
Main placement on square pad grid
Top rail is 5V_A on row A. Bottom rail is GND on row J. Lead endpoints and socket pins are placed on the same repeated board pitch.
This view ignores the rails and only shows the path you should wire first.
build around pin 3, pin 2, and pin 6 first
This view keeps the top 5V rail and bottom GND rail explicit so the clamp and decoupling placement is easier to follow.
Coordinates below follow the main board above. Where a capacitor lands above the bottom rail, its lower pad is then linked straight down to row J on the same column.
The board uses a top 5V rail and bottom GND rail. External DAC ground should land on row J.
B1 bridges to B2. B3 continues to B4 and then down to U1 pin 3 at G7.
D4 is linked straight down to J4. This keeps the part on a pad multiple while preserving the bottom ground rail.
The socket rows are aligned to the same square grid as the board pads. Pin 3 is G7, pin 2 is F7, pin 6 is G10, pin 7 is F10, and pin 4 is H7.
C9 then links down to J9. This keeps the capacitor close to the op-amp while the actual ground rail stays on the bottom row.
The feedback path starts at U1 pin 2 on F7, rises to row D, goes through R3, then enters the trim cluster.
This area is spread out so the trim body is not on top of R3, R2, or the clamp diodes.
R2 now visibly sits on board pads and drops from the trim/feedback node down to the bottom ground rail.
The op-amp output at G10 runs through R4, then continues right to the output clamp node on G15 and G16.
D4 reaches up to the top rail. D5 reaches down to the bottom rail. Both parts are placed on pad multiples that match the square board pitch.
